text.title: Retroactive ADR Workflow Worked Example
text.summary: Worked example showing the methodology applied end-to-end, with a trace through the rules and the final spec artefact.

section.title: Trace
section.p: Discovery digest week of 2026-05-18 surfaced three candidates:

list.item: repeated_question (×7): "why opaque tokens instead of JWT?"
onboarding_blocker (×3): "where is the Redis sized for?"
surprise_pr_review (×2): "auth flow assumes Redis is local"

section.p: Owner Jonas picks the top candidate ("opaque tokens vs JWT") and drafts a retro-ADR using `templates/retro-adr.md`. Sources cited: PR #312, chat thread #auth 2025-09-12, commit 7f3a9b2. The body splits original_intent (cited from PR comments at the time) from current_reconstruction (Redis cost analysis dated 2026-04). Single owner signs off; reviewer checks r1 (no blame), r3 (split honest), r5 (single owner).

section.title: Final artefact
section.code: { "id": "ADR-0042", "title": "Authentication uses opaque tokens, not JWT", "status": "accepted-retroactively", "decided_on_estimated": "q3-2025", "authored_on": "2026-05-20", "owner": "jonas@co", "sources": [ "PR #312", "chat thread #auth 2025-09-12", "commit 7f3a9b2" ], "body": { "original_intent": "Opaque tokens were chosen because the auth flow already terminated at our gateway and JWT validation would have required a sidecar per service.", "current_reconstruction": "Opaque tokens shifted validation cost to a Redis lookup per request, fine at \u2264200 RPS, problematic at horizontal shard scale.", "consequences": "Redis becomes a critical dependency; outage = auth outage.", "alternatives_considered_now": "JWT + JWKS removes Redis dependency at the cost of refresh logic per service." } }

list.item: r1 satisfied: passive voice; no blame field.
r3 satisfied: original_intent and current_reconstruction live in separate sections with sources.
r5 satisfied: single named owner jonas@co.
